summary: The user is planning a road trip from their hometown to visit national parks out west, specifically expressing interest in the Grand Teton and Yellowstone Loop. They are considering camping at Colter Bay Campground and inquired about electric hookups, potable water, and dump station availability.
facts: The user recently drove for six hours to Washington D.C. The user is planning a road trip for 7-10 days to visit national parks out west. The user prefers scenic routes that go through national parks or have nice views. The user is considering camping at Colter Bay Campground in Grand Teton National Park. Colter Bay Campground has 350 campsites, with 100 offering electric hookups. The user plans to reserve an electric campsite at Colter Bay Campground through recreation.gov. Potable water stations are available throughout Colter Bay Campground for refilling RV water tanks. The dump station at Colter Bay Campground is free for campers and located near the campground's entrance. The user is advised to conserve water while using the facilities at Colter Bay Campground.
